questions.question: Explain the Read (Load) and Write (Store) memory operations with the help of diagrams and Register Transfer Notation. (5 marks)
keyPoints: Read operation: Processor places address on address bus, activates Read signal, memory places data on data bus, processor reads into register. RTN: R ← [LOC].
Write operation: Processor places address on address bus, places data on data bus, activates Write signal, memory stores data. RTN: [LOC] ← R.
Both operations use three buses: address bus, data bus, and control bus.

questions.question: Explain the concept of memory access time, word alignment, and how memory is accessed during instruction execution (fetch, operand read, result store). (10 marks)
keyPoints: Memory access time: Time from initiation of Read/Write to completion. SRAM ~5-10 ns, DRAM ~50-100 ns.
Memory hierarchy: Registers (fastest, smallest) → Cache → Main Memory → Secondary Storage (slowest, largest).
Word alignment: A word is aligned when its address is a multiple of the word size. Aligned access needs one memory operation; unaligned may need two.
Instruction fetch: Read instruction from memory at address in Program Counter (PC).
Operand fetch: Read data operand from memory if the instruction references a memory location.
Result store: Write computation result back to memory if the destination is a memory location.
Example: ADD R1, [1000] — fetches instruction, reads operand from address 1000, adds to R1 (result stays in register, no memory store needed).
